title: Job Openings at Oando Plc
content:
Oando Plc is one of Africa’s largest integrated energy solutions provider with a proud heritage. It has a primary listing on the Nigeria Stock Exchange and a secondary listing on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange. With shared values of Teamwork, Respect, Integrity, Passion and Professionalism (TRIPP).

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Corporate Finance Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To support the Head, Corporate Finance in executing the company’s capital-raising strategy across debt and equity markets and overall balance sheet optimization initiatives to achieve the goal of providing sustainable value to shareholders.


Key Responsibilities Areas (KRAs) and Initiatives
Capital Raising & Transaction Execution:


	Support the structuring and execution of capital raises through equity, debt, and hybrid instruments.
	Assist in rights issues, private placements, project finance, reserve-based lending (RBL), and other capital market transactions.
	Prepare financial models, valuation analyses, and funding structures for upstream assets and projects.
	Coordinate due diligence processes and manage transaction timelines.
	Liaise with investment banks, lenders, legal advisers, financial advisers, and regulators


Financial Analysis & Modelling:


	Develop and maintain integrated financial models for upstream operations and joint ventures. This will also include other businesses in the Group, including trading and mining.
	Conduct project economics, cash flow forecasting, and scenario/sensitivity analysis.
	Evaluate financing options and assess impact on capital structure and shareholder value.
	Support preparation of investment memoranda presentations and credit papers.
	Develop financial models to support drilling campaigns, field development plans, and work programmes.
	Evaluate project economics including production profiles, lifting costs, capex plans, and sensitivity analysis.
	Conduct valuation analysis (DCF, NAV, transaction comparables).
	Perform capital structure analysis, refinancing assessments, and covenant monitoring.


Investor & Stakeholder Engagement:


	Assist with investor presentations, information memoranda, and offering documents.
	Support engagement with institutional investors, DFIs, and commercial lenders.
	Help manage data rooms and respond to investor and lender queries.


Strategic & Corporate Finance Support:


	Support M&A analysis, farm-in/farm-out transactions, and asset acquisitions/divestments.
	Contribute to corporate strategy, capital allocation, and funding planning.
	Monitor market conditions, financing trends, and competitor transactions.


Governance & Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements (e.g., SEC, stock exchange, lenders).
	Support preparation of board and management approval materials.
	Maintain transaction documentation and internal approvals


Qualifications and Experience Requirements
Minimum Qualification:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Engineering, or related fields.


Required Professional Certifications / Accreditations / Registrations:


	Professional certifications in finance such ACA, ACCA, CFA, is mandatory. MBA is an added advantage.


Minimum Years of Experience and Relevant Areas:


	7 - 10 years of experience in corporate finance, investment banking, private equity, or upstream oil & gas finance.


Any Additional Qualifications / Certifications or Experience (Desirable but not essential):


	MBA or Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, or related discipline


Behavioral and Technical Competencies Required
Behavioral Competencies:


	Stakeholder Engagement & Relationship Management
	Communication & Presentation Skills
	Attention to Detail
	Networking & Influence
	Adaptability & Resilience
	Problem-Solving & Critical Thinking
	Integrity & Ethical Practice
	Results Orientation.


Technical Competencies:


	Oil & Gas Industry & Asset Economics Knowledge
	Financial Modelling & Project Evaluation
	Capital Raising & Transaction Execution
	Market & Investor Intelligence.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) Facility Management Supervisor

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-time 
Reports to: Facilities Manager
Indirect Reports: Facilities Officers / Technicians and Administrative Support

Job Summary


	To supervise and support in planning, coordinating, and overseeing the effective operation, maintenance, and safety of all assigned facilities operations, assets, and service providers for the organization, in order to ensure safe, compliant, cost-effective, reliable workplace environments, and uninterrupted business operations.
	The role requires strong operational oversight, vendor management, stakeholder engagement, problem-solving skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment.


Key Responsibilities Areas (KRAs) & Initiatives
Facilities Operations and Maintenance Management:


	Supervise daily facilities operations, inspections, and maintenance activities through structured schedules and checklists to ensure assets remain functional, safe, and compliant with standards, preventing downtime and service disruptions.
	Coordinate preventive and corrective maintenance plans with internal teams and vendors to extend asset life, reduce breakdowns, and optimize lifecycle costs that protect operational continuity.
	Monitor service delivery against agreed Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and escalate deviations promptly to ensure timely resolution and consistent service quality.
	Maintain accurate facilities logs, asset registers, and maintenance records to support audit readiness, traceability, and informed decision-making.
	Support emergency response and incident management activities to minimize risk exposure and ensure rapid restoration of services.


Space and Asset Management:


	Support space planning and utilization, including office allocation, seating arrangements, and workspace optimization.
	Maintain an up-to-date inventory of facility assets, furniture, and equipment.
	Coordinate the movement, installation, or disposal of office assets as required.
	Support facility expansion, relocation, or renovation projects when needed.


Vendor and Contract Performance Management:


	Supervise third-party service providers through regular performance reviews, site inspections, and feedback sessions to ensure contractual compliance and value for money.
	Track contract milestones, renewals, and obligations using structured tools to prevent service lapses, cost overruns, or compliance risks.
	Validate vendor invoices and service reports against delivered work to ensure accuracy, cost control, and financial integrity.
	Support procurement and sourcing activities by providing technical input and performance insights that strengthen vendor selection and negotiation outcomes.
	Address non-performance issues through documented escalations and corrective action plans to protect service reliability and organizational standards.


Health, Safety, Environment, and Compliance Oversight:


	Enforce Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) standards and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) across all facilities activities to reduce incidents and regulatory exposure.
	Conduct routine safety inspections and risk assessments to identify hazards early and implement corrective measures that safeguard employees and assets.
	Support statutory inspections, certifications, and regulatory requirements by coordinating documentation and site readiness to ensure zero compliance defaults.
	Promote a safety-conscious culture by engaging staff and vendors on safe work practices and incident reporting.
	Investigate facilities-related incidents and near-misses to identify root causes and implement preventive controls.


Financial Control and Cost Optimization:


	Monitor facilities budgets and operational spend against approved limits to ensure cost discipline and transparency.
	Identify cost-saving opportunities through data analysis, preventive maintenance planning, and process improvements that enhance efficiency without compromising service quality.
	Support financial reporting by providing accurate facilities cost data and explanations to management.
	Track utilities and consumables usage to identify trends, variances, and optimization opportunities.
	Contribute to annual budget planning and forecasting to align facilities costs with organizational priorities.


Team Supervision and Stakeholder Engagement:


	Supervise and support facilities staff through task allocation, coaching, and performance feedback to build accountability and capability.
	Coordinate with internal stakeholders to understand service needs, manage expectations, and resolve issues proactively.
	Facilitate regular team and vendor meetings to review performance, address challenges, and align priorities.
	Provide clear communication and reporting to management on facilities performance, risks, and improvement initiatives.
	Support change initiatives and continuous improvement efforts that enhance service delivery and user experience.


Qualifications & Experience Requirements
Minimum Qualification:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering, Facilities Management, Business Administration, or a related discipline.


Required Professional Certifications / Accreditations / Registrations:


	Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) certification (mandatory).


Minimum Years of Experience and Relevant Areas:


	Minimum of 5 years of relevant facilities or building management experience, including at least 2 years in a supervisory role.


Proven experience in:


	Facilities operations and maintenance coordination.
	Vendor and service provider supervision.
	Health, safety, and compliance monitoring.
	Budget tracking and cost control.
	Facilities reporting and documentation.


Any Additional Qualifications / Certifications or Experience (Desirable but not essential):


	Certification in Facilities Management (IFMA, BIFM, or equivalent).
	Experience in multi-site facilities operations.


Behavioural and Technical Competencies Requirements:
Behavioral Competencies:


	Leadership and People Supervision: Guides and supports team members through clear direction, feedback, and accountability to achieve consistent performance.
	Stakeholder Engagement: Builds productive working relationships with internal users and vendors to resolve issues and align expectations.
	Problem-Solving and Decision-Making: Identifies operational challenges, analyses root causes, and implements practical solutions.
	Attention to Detail: Maintains accuracy in inspections, records, and reports to prevent errors and compliance gaps.
	Adaptability and Resilience: Responds effectively to changing priorities, constraints, and operational pressures.
	Integrity and Accountability: Acts transparently and responsibly in managing resources, vendors, and information.
	Communication Skills: Shares information clearly and timely with teams and management.
	Results Orientation: Focuses on delivering measurable service and operational outcomes.


Technical Competencies:


	Facilities Operations Management: Applies knowledge of building systems, maintenance practices, and service coordination.
	Health, Safety & Environment (HSE): Implements safety standards, risk assessments, and compliance requirements.
	Vendor and Contract Administration: Monitors service delivery, contract terms, and performance metrics.
	Budget Monitoring and Cost Control: Tracks spend and identifies efficiency opportunities.
	Asset and Maintenance Planning: Manages asset registers and preventive maintenance programs.
	Incident and Risk Management: Investigates incidents and applies corrective controls.
	Reporting and Documentation: Produces structured reports and maintains audit-ready records.
	Digital Systems Utilization: Uses work-order systems, spreadsheets, and document management tools to track facilities activities.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Bridgemead Consulting is a holistic and innovative business consultancy that believes in connecting businesses with the right individuals. We take pride in not just connecting clients to skilled talent but also ensuring we provide personalised solutions to all Human Resource challenges.

At Bridgemead we offer professional and unique Human Resources solutions to facilitate specific business requirements, enabling you focus solely on your business. We are a fully outsourced platform for Small Medium Enterprises (SME&rsquo;s) and our services span the full spectrum of all HR relayed business priorities. All our experts are CIPM, CIPD, SPHRI qualified with vast experience of real &ndash; life business processes.

Our team of dedicated professionals guarantee efficient, attainable and effective results that will positively transform your business processes internally and externally irrespective of size.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Branch Manager (Microfinance)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations / Retail Banking
Level: Management
Reports To: Regional Manager

Role Summary


	The Branch Manager will drive business growth, profitability, risk control, and operational excellence within the branch, while ensuring regulatory compliance and strong customer relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute branch business strategy to meet loan, deposit, and revenue targets.
	Drive deposit mobilization, credit growth, and product cross-selling.
	Manage loan processing, monitoring, and recovery; maintain PAR &le; 5%.
	Ensure compliance with CBN regulations, KYC, AML/CFT, and internal controls.
	Oversee daily branch operations and service delivery standards.
	Identify and mitigate credit and operational risks.
	Lead and develop staff; manage performance and discipline processes.
	Build customer and community partnerships to grow market share.
	Monitor branch performance and submit accurate periodic reports.
	Improve processes and support digital/operational transformation.


KPIs


	&ge;90% loan and deposit target achievement
	PAR &le; 5%
	&ge;85% customer retention
	Profitability targets met
	&ge;95% recovery on performing loans
	100% compliance and reporting timeliness
	&ge;99% reporting accuracy
	Strong audit/internal control ratings


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or related field (MBA is an advantage).
	5&ndash;7 years of banking experience, with at least 3 years in management.
	Solid knowledge of microfinance operations, credit, and regulation.
	Strong leadership, analytical, and stakeholder management skills.


Core Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strategic planning, team leadership, business development, risk &amp; compliance oversight, negotiation, communication, problem-solving, and customer focus.


Salary
N350,000&nbsp;- N450,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;career.bridgemeadnghr@gmail.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&ldquo;Branch Manager (Microfinance)&rdquo; as the email subject of the mail.

Branch Manager (Microfinance) Job at Bridgemead Consulting




        
    

        
        Bridgemead Consulting is a holistic and innovative business consultancy that believes in connecting businesses with the right individuals. We take pride in not just connecting clients to skilled talent but also ensuring we provide personalised solutions to all Human Resource challenges.

At Bridgemead we offer professional and unique Human Resources solutions to facilitate specific business requirements, enabling you focus solely on your business. We are a fully outsourced platform for Small Medium Enterprises (SME’s) and our services span the full spectrum of all HR relayed business priorities. All our experts are CIPM, CIPD, SPHRI qualified with vast experience of real – life business processes.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our team of dedicated professionals guarantee efficient, attainable and effective results that will positively transform your business processes internally and externally irrespective of size.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Branch Manager (Microfinance)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations / Retail Banking
Level: Management
Reports To: Regional Manager

Role Summary


	The Branch Manager will drive business growth, profitability, risk control, and operational excellence within the branch, while ensuring regulatory compliance and strong customer relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Execute branch business strategy to meet loan, deposit, and revenue targets.
	Drive deposit mobilization, credit growth, and product cross-selling.
	Manage loan processing, monitoring, and recovery; maintain PAR ≤ 5%.
	Ensure compliance with CBN regulations, KYC, AML/CFT, and internal controls.
	Oversee daily branch operations and service delivery standards.
	Identify and mitigate credit and operational risks.
	Lead and develop staff; manage performance and discipline processes.
	Build customer and community partnerships to grow market share.
	Monitor branch performance and submit accurate periodic reports.
	Improve processes and support digital/operational transformation.


KPIs


	≥90% loan and deposit target achievement
	PAR ≤ 5%
	≥85% customer retention
	Profitability targets met
	≥95% recovery on performing loans
	100% compliance and reporting timeliness
	≥99% reporting accuracy
	Strong audit/internal control ratings


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or related field (MBA is an advantage).
	5–7 years of banking experience, with at least 3 years in management.
	Solid knowledge of microfinance operations, credit, and regulation.
	Strong leadership, analytical, and stakeholder management skills.


Core Skills & Competencies:


	Strategic planning, team leadership, business development, risk & compliance oversight, negotiation, communication, problem-solving, and customer focus.


Salary
N350,000 - N450,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Branch Manager (Microfinance)” as the email subject of the mail.

Note: Qualified Candidates only.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development (Fintech)",
  "description": "Easy Buy Limited&nbsp;- We are a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria.&nbsp;Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.

Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development (Fintech)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales growth by ensuringtargets are met.
	Mobilize new customers and grow assigned territories.
	Conduct consistent follow-up on clients to ensure low default rate.
	Meet and exceed assigned weekly and monthly sales targets.
	Maintain accurate records of customer accounts and repayment schedules
	Build and sustain strong customer relationships within assigned communities


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 1 &ndash; 2 years of experience in Sales, Field Marketing, or Direct Sales (fintech experience is an advantage)
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Target-driven with proven sales performance record
	Basic reporting and documentation skills.


Benefits


	Salary:&nbsp;N150,000 monthly.
	Performance Bonus: Up to 150,000 Monthly.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;humanresources.prisc@outlook.com&nbsp;using &quot;Business Developer &ndash; Ibadan&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development (Fintech) Job at Easy Buy Limited




        
    

        
        Easy Buy Limited - We are a fast-growing financial services organization operating under a duly licensed finance company regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN). Since our establishment in 2019, the company has built a strong reputation for delivering innovative and accessible financial solutions that empower individuals and businesses across Nigeria. Through one of our dynamic consumer-focused platforms, we provide flexible financing solutions, including asset financing and other customer-centric financial products designed to improve access to essential goods and services. Our solutions are particularly tailored to meet the needs of underserved and emerging markets, helping customers achieve their goals through convenient and structured payment plans.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Backed by a team of forward-thinking financial professionals and industry experts, the organization combines deep market insight with strong financial expertise to design tailor-made lending, investment, and financial advisory solutions for clients across both the private and public sectors.As we continue to expand our footprint and impact, we remain committed to innovation, integrity, and financial inclusion, providing opportunities for talented professionals who are passionate about growth, performance, and making a meaningful difference in the financial services landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development (Fintech)

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales growth by ensuringtargets are met.
	Mobilize new customers and grow assigned territories.
	Conduct consistent follow-up on clients to ensure low default rate.
	Meet and exceed assigned weekly and monthly sales targets.
	Maintain accurate records of customer accounts and repayment schedules
	Build and sustain strong customer relationships within assigned communities


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 1 – 2 years of experience in Sales, Field Marketing, or Direct Sales (fintech experience is an advantage)
	Strong persuasion, negotiation, and interpersonal skills
	Target-driven with proven sales performance record
	Basic reporting and documentation skills.


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 monthly.
	Performance Bonus: Up to 150,000 Monthly.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Business Developer – Ibadan" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at HRHub.ng Limited
content:
HRHub.ng Limited is a Human Resource Development & Management Consulting practice firm located in Lagos. Since its founding, HRHub.ng Ltd has grown to become a leader in the provision of high value human resources, business advisory as well as learning & development solutions to multinationals, local corporate organizations, governments and multilateral organizations. Our depth of our expertise, defined by carefully selected pool of multi-disciplinary consultants, ensures that we continue to be the firm of choice to our growing list of discerning clienteles.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Housekeeper
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a reliable and hardworking Housekeeper to maintain cleanliness and organization in the home.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring a clean, comfortable, and well-maintained living environment.
	Accommodation will be provided for the successful candidate.


Key Responsibilities


	Clean and maintain all areas of the home including bedrooms, bathrooms, kitchen, and living spaces.
	Perform daily housekeeping tasks such as sweeping, mopping, dusting, and laundry.
	Change bed linens and ensure rooms are tidy and well arranged.
	Properly handle and store cleaning supplies and equipment.
	Assist with basic household errands when required.
	Ensure a high standard of hygiene and cleanliness at all times.
	Report any maintenance issues or damages in the home.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Proven experience as a housekeeper or in a similar role is an advantage.
	Ability to maintain a high level of cleanliness and organization.
	Trustworthy, responsible, and hardworking.
	Good time management and attention to detail.
	Ability to follow instructions and work independently.


Benefits
Accommodation will be provided.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Cook

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a skilled and reliable Cook to prepare high-quality meals while maintaining cleanliness and organization in the kitchen.
	The ideal candidate should be knowledgeable in meal preparation, kitchen hygiene, and food safety standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and cook a variety of meals according to recipes or customer preferences.
	Ensure food is prepared and presented in an appealing and hygienic manner.
	Plan menus and assist in creating new meal ideas when necessary.
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the kitchen and cooking equipment.
	Monitor food stock levels and inform management of items that need restocking.
	Ensure proper storage and handling of food items.
	Follow all food safety and hygiene regulations.
	Assist with grocery shopping or ingredient sourcing when required.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Proven experience as a cook or in a similar role.
	Knowledge of various cooking methods, ingredients, and kitchen equipment.
	Ability to maintain high standards of cleanliness and food safety.
	Good time management and organizational skills.
	Ability to work independently and follow instructions.
	Culinary certification is an added advantage.


Salary
N90,000 - N100,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
